title: Computer Vision AI Engineer
description:
Computer Vision AI Engineer

The Opportunity:

Booz Allen Hamilton is seeking an innovative and experienced
AI Developer specializing in Computer Vision to join our
growing team for Space solutions. In this role, you will
leverage your expertise in artificial intelligence, data
science, and machine learning engineering to train, test,
deploy, and maintain models that learn from data.

The successful candidate will collaborate with cross-
functional teams to develop and deploy advanced computer
vision models and systems, bringing significant
technological advancements that drive mission success.

In this role, you’ll contribute to the direction of mission-
critical solutions by applying best-fit ML algorithms and
technologies. You’ll grow within a large community of
machine learning engineers across the company and
collaborate with a team of dedicated Space industry and AI
professionals to deliver world-class solutions to solve
missile warning problems in near-real-time.

What You'll Work On:

- Develop and implement deep learning computer vision models,
  with a focus on sensor fusion and target tracking.
- Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to design, develop,
  test, and deploy technical solutions in Python, C++, and
  Rust.
- Develop and maintain Fire Control Systems, ensuring
  robustness and accuracy in real-time applications.
- Utilize GPU programming, including CUDA or RAPIDs, to
  optimize the performance of computer vision applications.
- Contribute to the architecture and implementation of
  embedded systems programming using C, C++, or Rust.

Join us. The world can’t wait.

You Have: 

- 5+ years of experience in artificial intelligence, data
  science, machine learning engineering, or software
  engineering
- Experience in deep learning for computer vision, including
  the use of transformer-based models such as DINO, CLIP, or
  SAM
- Experience in sensor fusion and target tracking
- Experience with developing technical solutions using Python,
  C++, or Rust
- Ability to obtain a Secret clearance
- Bachelor's degree in Science, Technology, Engineering, or
  Mathematics

Nice If You Have: 

- Experience with embedded systems programming in C, C++, or
  Rust
- Experience in GPU programming, including CUDA or RAPIDs
- Experience with developing in-space solutions
- Knowledge of modern software design patterns, including
  micro-service design and orchestration in Kubernetes
  deployment
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Artificial
  Intelligence, or a related field

Clearance:

Applicants selected will be subject to a security
investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements
for access to classified information.

Compensation

At Booz Allen, we celebrate your contributions, provide you
with opportunities and choices, and support your total well-
being. Our offerings include health, life, disability,
financial, and retirement benefits, as well as paid leave,
professional development, tuition assistance, work-life
programs, and dependent care. Our recognition awards program
acknowledges employees for exceptional performance and
superior demonstration of our values. Full-time and part-
time employees working at least 20 hours a week on a regular
basis are eligible to participate in Booz Allen’s benefit
programs. Individuals that do not meet the threshold are
only eligible for select offerings, not inclusive of health
benefits. We encourage you to learn more about our total
benefits by visiting the Resource page on our Careers site
and reviewing Our Employee Benefits page.

Salary at Booz Allen is determined by various factors,
including but not limited to location, the individual’s
particular combination of education, knowledge, skills,
competencies, and experience, as well as contract-specific
affordability and organizational requirements. The projected
compensation range for this position is $77,500.00 to
$176,000.00 (annualized USD). The estimate displayed
represents the typical salary range for this position and is
just one component of Booz Allen’s total compensation
package for employees. This posting will close within 90
days from the Posting Date.

Identity Statement

As part of the application process, you are expected to be
on camera during interviews and assessments. We reserve the
right to take your picture to verify your identity and
prevent fraud.

Work Model Our people-first culture prioritizes the benefits
of flexibility and collaboration, whether that happens in
person or remotely.

- If this position is listed as remote or hybrid, you’ll
  periodically work from a Booz Allen or client site
  facility.
- If this position is listed as onsite, you’ll work with
  colleagues and clients in person, as needed for the
  specific role.

Commitment to Non-Discrimination

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for
employment without regard to disability, status as a
protected veteran or any other status protected by
applicable federal, state, local, or international law.
responsibilities: Develop and implement deep learning computer vision models with focus on sensor fusion and target tracking
Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to design, develop, test, and deploy technical solutions in Python, C++, and Rust
Develop and maintain Fire Control Systems ensuring robustness and accuracy in real-time applications
Utilize GPU programming including CUDA or RAPIDs to optimize performance of computer vision applications
Contribute to architecture and implementation of embedded systems programming using C, C++, or Rust
use_cases: Fire control systems for real-time applications
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role focuses on traditional computer vision and deep
learning models (DINO, CLIP, SAM) for sensor fusion and
target tracking, not generative AI or LLMs. The engineer
will train, test, deploy, and optimize these CV models using
GPU programming and embedded systems - classic ML/DL work
applied to computer vision domains.
